input.background: A group of 115,818 women between 30 and 55 years of age were recruited to be in a study. Individuals were free of coronary heart disease at the time of recruitment. Body weight of subjects was determined as of 1976 (WT_1976) and also as of age 18 (WT_18). From these variables, weight change (WT_CHNG = WT_1976 - WT_18) was calculated. Adult height in meters (H1) was determined and used to calculate body mass index (BMI) using the formula: BMI = weight in kilograms ÷ (height in meters)^2. BMI was determined as of age 18 (BMI_18) and at the time of recruitment in 1976 (BMI_1976). BMI was classified into quintiles, dividing a quantitative measurement into five ordered categories with an equal number of individuals in each group. The quintile cutoff points for BMI at age 18 were: ≤19.1, 19.1–20.3, 20.4–21.5, 21.6–23.2, and ≥23.3. This information was encoded into a new ordinal variable BM_18_GRP with values 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 for each quintile. The study followed individuals over time and monitored whether they experienced adverse coronary events. A new categorical variable, CORONARY, was used to record whether a person experienced an adverse coronary event. During the first 14 years of follow-up, there were 1292 such events.
input.question: How was BMI calculated, and what were the quintile cutoff points for BMI at age 18?

output.answer: BMI was calculated using the formula: BMI = weight in kilograms ÷ (height in meters)^2. The quintile cutoff points for BMI at age 18 were: ≤19.1, 19.1–20.3, 20.4–21.5, 21.6–23.2, and ≥23.3.

meta_info.caption: Weight change and coronary heart disease.
